user_question: What is HFpEF and what are its symptoms?
final_answer:
HFpEF Treatment Options and Medications The management of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) focuses on controlling symptoms, treating the underlying conditions that drive it (such as high blood pressure, diabetes, obesity, and kidney disease), and reducing the chance of being hospitalized. There is no single cure; treatment is usually a combination of medication and lifestyle changes that a care team adjusts over time. Safety Warnings: Emergency Symptoms and When to Seek Help People living with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) and related cardio-kidney-metabolic conditions should know the difference between routine changes that can be managed with the care team and emergency symptoms that need immediate help. This information is general education and does not replace professional medical advice.

user_question: What medications help reduce hospitalizations in HFpEF?
final_answer:
HFpEF Treatment Options and Medications The management of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) focuses on controlling symptoms, treating the underlying conditions that drive it (such as high blood pressure, diabetes, obesity, and kidney disease), and reducing the chance of being hospitalized. There is no single cure; treatment is usually a combination of medication and lifestyle changes that a care team adjusts over time. Patient Education: Daily Living and Self-Monitoring with HFpEF Living well with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) involves daily habits that help keep fluid balance steady and reduce strain on the heart. These habits work alongside medicines, not instead of them, and they help people notice early when something is changing.
